title: Find a Service Canada Outreach activity near you - Canada.ca
content:
## Find a Service Canada Outreach activity near you
Service Canada expands its reach by offering outreach activities within communities. If you need information on or help applying for Service Canada programs or benefits such as the Social Insurance Number, Canadian Dental Care Plan, Employment Insurance, Canada Pension Plan, Old Age Security and other Government of Canada programs and services but are unable or prefer not to travel to a Service Canada office, you can consult this page to find upcoming outreach events in your community.

## How to find a Service Canada outreach activity
Below you will find a list of upcoming outreach events.  You can narrow your search by filtering activities by:
- delivery mode (either in-person or virtually)
Outreach activities are scheduled based on the availability of host organizations and are planned regularly.  Check the listing of activities regularly for new activities and potential changes to those already listed.
Certain activities require registration, which can be completed using the contact information provided or through the ‟Register” button which may redirect to an external website.
Learn how your privacy is protected if you need to register for a Service Canada outreach activity:

## Privacy Notice Statement and Disclaimer

### Privacy Notice Statement – Registration for a Service Canada outreach activity by Service Canada or the host organization
The collection and use of your personal information is authorized under the Department of Employment and Social Development Act and other applicable laws. The information you provide will be used by Service Canada or the  organization hosting the activity to contact you by phone call and/or by email if you registered for an event. Registration for this Service Canada outreach activity is voluntary. Not providing information will result in Service Canada being unable to register you for the activity and unable to contact you should the activity be modified, rescheduled, or cancelled.
The information you provide may be used and/or disclosed for policy analysis, research and/or evaluation purposes. However, these additional uses and/or disclosures of your personal information will never result in an administrative decision being made about benefits you may be entitled to.
The information you provide is administered in accordance with the Department of Employment and Social Development Act , Privacy Act , and other applicable laws. You have the right to the protection of, access to, and correction of your personal information, which is described in Personal Information Bank PSU 938 “Outreach Activities”. Instructions for obtaining this information are outlined in the government publication: Info Source .
Info Source may also be accessed on-line at any Service Canada Centre. If you are not satisfied with our response to your privacy, you have the right to file a complaint with the Privacy Commissioner of Canada regarding Service Canada’s handling of their personal information at: Report a concern .
When registering for a Service Canada outreach activity, you may be redirected to a website outside of the Government of Canada. The information is provided by the organizations voluntarily. Please note that these sites are external and not owned by the Government of Canada. The Government of Canada is not responsible for any of the information or content.
Visiting these external websites means you will be subject to the websites’ own:
- terms and conditions, and
Please visit the Canada.ca Terms and conditions and Privacy pages for more information.
Learn more about health measures before attending an outreach activity:

## Health measures and other considerations for Service Canada in-person outreach activities

### Mask-Friendly Environment
Service Canada offers a mask-friendly environment. Clients should feel comfortable to wear a mask during Service Canada outreach events.

### Don't attend if you:
- are experiencing any symptoms associated with COVID-19 (even if mild)
- have tested positive on a PCR or rapid test, been presumed positive for COVID-19 in the past 5 days
- have been instructed by public health authorities or a health care provider to quarantine or isolate for any reason

### Limit the use of scented products
Service Canada promotes a scent reduced work environment and encourages the limited use of scented products at Service Canada outreach events.
Check here for upcoming Service Canada outreach activities. Information is updated regularly, so please check back often.
